index.title: index
index.content:
Welcome to my cooking vault!
On this public version you can find my collection of recipes, ingredients, and cookbooks (inspired by Frank’s cooking). This is just the public facing arm of a larger Obsidian vault where I manage my grocery list (see this guide) and keep track of restaurants I liked - or hated so much it was noteworthy.
Browse recipes by the categories below, or click through the links to explore

Cuisine
Protein
Carb
slow
Weeknight Faves

Cooking as a database
The beauty of using Obsidian for this kind of thing is that I can attach metadata to each recipe or ingredient note. This allows me to query notes as if they were in a big database, which can be used to look at my cooking knowledge from several different angles
For example, I can see all my recipes for a certain cuisine:
Transclude of japanese
all the lunch recipes under 20 mins long:





















FilelengthLamb burger15Tuna Melt10bbq pork bun10
or I can plan a meal around a single ingredient in my fridge, e.g. egg, asparagus, carrot
Tweaking to my taste
When making a recipe the creator will try and find the combination of ingredients that will work best for most people. But I am not cooking for most people, I am cooking for myself.
The internet is full of different ways to achieve the same end goal. Its always funny to see one youtube video say “you must absolutely avoid this” after just seeing the same tip in another video. After trying to recipe out for myself, I will include a embedded link to my favourite website and write warnings to myself if I messed something up. In this way, I can keep building on my skills and technique and not accidentally create a chemical weapon by putting 3 tbsp, instead of 3 tsp, of fish sauce in my Thai green curry.
Restaurants
How many times have you taken a picture of a restaurant dish and left if lost in your cluttered camera roll? Since Obsidian keeps track of all the links between files, I can have a dataview query to show all the times I have had this meal in a restaurant. That way I can judge if I have hit RQ (restaurant quality).
I have written mini reviews for each restaurant, but for this public website I am only showing the metadata of the page. You can still see my Restaurant rankings
Groceries
Each recipe has its own list of ingredients set out like
ingredients

 Bolognese
 bechemel
 pasta
 mozzarella
 parmesan

This means they can be collected and shown by the Checklist plugin. This setup also works really well for keeping track of the Pantry, if I run out of something I just untick it from the list and it will automatically pop up when I go to buy the ingredients for some recipe…magic

ingredients/fish.title: Fish
ingredients/fish.content:
Need to rinse fish before cooking to remove the “fishy” odors on the surface 
                  
                

To balance the salt in seawater, fish have amino acids like glycine (sweet) and glutamic acid (umami)
Sharks use urea instead which obviously doesn’t taste great
Because fish live in cold waters, their unsaturated fat spoils quicker when on surface. Also the bacteria that live in them are used to the cold temperature of the fridge so continue to spoil
Pg 188 of On food and Cooking

ingredients/jasmine-rice.title: Jasmine rice
ingredients/jasmine-rice.content:
When buying it should say Hom Mali on it, which means jasmine rice in Thai. Technically, Jasmine rice is not a protected phrase
1 part rice 1 part water
Wash 2 times (want a bit of starch) then add water and bring to simmer - then heat down and time 10mins
Wait a bit longer if water not evaporated
Leave with lid on for another 10 mins
Take lid off and stir up and leave for another 10 mins

ingredients/plan.title: Plan
ingredients/plan.content:
Welcome to my cooking vault!
On this public version you can find my collection of recipes, ingredients, and cookbooks (inspired by Frank’s cooking). This is just the public facing arm of a larger Obsidian vault where I manage my grocery list (see this guide) and keep track of restaurants I liked (or hated so much it was noteworthy).
Cooking as a database
The beauty of using Obsidian for this kind of thing is that I can attach metadata to each recipe or ingredient note. This allows me to query notes as if they were in a big database, which can be used to look at my cooking knowledge from several different angles
For example, I can see all my recipes for a certain cuisine:
Transclude of japanese
all the lunch recipes under 20 mins long:





















FilelengthLamb burger15Tuna Melt10bbq pork bun10
or I can plan a meal around a single ingredient in my fridge:
Transclude of asparagus
Tweaking to my taste
When making a recipe the creator will try and find the combination of ingredients that will work best for most people. But I am not cooking for most people, I am cooking for myself.
The internet is full of different ways to achieve the same end goal. Its always funny to see one youtube video say “you must absolutely avoid this” after just seeing the same tip in another video. After trying to recipe out for myself, I will include a embedded link to my favourite website and write warnings to myself if I messed something up. In this way, I can keep building on my skills and technique and not accidentally create a chemical weapon by putting 3 tbsp, instead of 3 tsp, of fish sauce in my Thai green curry.
Restaurants
How many times have you taken a picture of a restaurant dish and left if lost in your cluttered camera roll? Since Obsidian keeps track of all the links between files, I can have a dataview query to show all the times I have had this meal in a restaurant. That way I can judge if I have hit RQ (restaurant quality).
e.g. restaurants with Egg fried rice





























FileRatingGold mine5Wing Tai5Fusjon4.5Royal China3.5Tian Fu3.5
Groceries
Each recipe has its own list of ingredients set out like
ingredients

 Bolognese
 béchemel
 pasta
 mozzarella
 parmesan

This means they can be collected and shown by the Checklist plugin. This setup also works really well for keeping track of the Pantry, if I run out of something I just untick it from the list and it will automatically pop up when I go to buy the ingredients for some recipe…magic

ingredients/prawns.title: Prawns
ingredients/prawns.content:
Pg 219 of On food and Cooking, the liver has enzymes that turn flesh into mush. Therefore normally sold without the head to stop this happening
Like Fish, they have glycine in their cells to balance salt. But they happen to have more than fish, which is why they taste sweeter

ingredients/salmon.title: Salmon
ingredients/salmon.content:
Cook to 45C

skin side down first for 90% of time, then 10% on other side
Or

Have leftovers cold with spring onion and sesame oil
Salmon store energy reserves in muscle not fat, after they have used these up the flesh will be mushy. So fisherman target them at the mouth of the river as they try and swim upstream to mate

ingredients/cheese.title: cheese
ingredients/cheese.content:
Formed by curdling milk and removing moisture. The most complex cheese come from West and North Europe because it is cold enough to enable a longer ageing process
Golden age of Cheese was 19th and 20th century until WW2 destroyed much of the pastures - then replaced with factories instead of local dairies.
Species:

Cow - neutral flavour
Sheep/Buffalo - richer in fat
Goat - low casein content, so doesn’t curdle as well, therefore crumbly cheeses

In France look for a affineurs - they buy cheese a bit young and then age it to perfection for selling
Storage
pg. 64 of On food and Cooking
My favourites

Stilton
Tellagio - rind washed cheese with Orange outside. See pg. 58 of On food and Cooking. Made with the same bacteria found on human skin so can smell a bit sweaty!
Port Salut - I think the above point is why slightly old port salut tasted like tallagio, it was the same bacteria that created the orange rind.
Cheddar - 3 year cornish gold from M&S
Pié D’Angloys - rich creamy with slight funk. Can get in M&S and Waitrose
tomme blanche (not sure which region, had in 2024 Paris)
Coulommiers on the ski trip - nice and milky without being too funky. Turns out it’s a type of Brie pg 83 of The Food of France


Stilcherton was a bit too strong for me and had that “death” smell from Camembert











FileBurgerCauliflower cheesebéchemel

ingredients/corn-tortillas.title: corn tortillas
ingredients/corn-tortillas.content:
Videos on how to make from fresh:
youtu.be/fKX7qHCpcw4
youtu.be/3J9mqTC6MnM

Heat up store bought:

Microwave in a wet palaver towel for 30-60 seconds
Put in tin foil to continue steaming for at least 5 mins (up to 15 mins)
Take out and place two on top on the meat to steam again
Take off and fry in hot oil while melting the cheese
Top with meat and toppings
Eat immediately


Best method by Rick Bayless using a microwave. Or this  video
much softer than frying and they have a pocket of steam in the middle
Frying doesn’t work for me but could try 25 seconds, 30 seconds, flip again for 20 seconds to puff up from this video. You need to dip in water first, to get the steam going
Ethan

Put in tin foil/food bag to steam and keep warm

ingredients/egg.title: egg
ingredients/egg.content:
On food and Cooking
Hens devote 25% of their daily energy to creating eggs. In a year of laying she will convert eight times her body weight into eggs.
It takes 25 hours to go from germ cell in the overlay of laying an egg.
Children shouldn’t have egg whites until they are 1 so that they don’t become allergic
Egg whites become tender at 65C and start to overcook at 80C. Liquids and sugar can alter these temperatures
Horrible grey ring around hard boiled eggs is where iron from yolk meets the sulfur from cooked egg white

ingredients/milk.title: milk
ingredients/milk.content:
Milk contains lactose which is not present in many other plants/animals. This means it takes time for bacteria to produce their own lactase to break down lactose into sugar so their can multiply. The exceptions are lactoabacilli and lactococci which get a head start on other microbes. They convert lactose into lactic acid which makes it hard for other microbes. So milk turns sour but becomes less hospitable for spoiling bacteria
Milk fat is contained in globules, which are surprisingly resistant to heat (unless turned acidic first). Milk and cream can be heated up for hours without splitting, but these globules can be broken by ice crystals when freezing.


                  
                  so don’t heat up previously frozen milk as the fat will split from the liquid 
                  
                

I hate the smell of warmed milk. Turns out that the lactoglobulin protein denatures at 78C which releases sulfur atoms which creates hydrogen sulphide gas that gives it the aroma! This is also present in UHT milk which is why it’s horrible
Milk will curdle if it’s too acidic, which happens if it’s too old as bacteria will create lactic acid

ingredients/red-wine.title: red wine
ingredients/red-wine.content:
French

Burgundy - smells like air fix glue but tastes nice! Can get very expensive
Beaujolais - fruity and smooth, great for drinking by itself
Malbec is just as fruity, I love it
Bordeaux is the best for me, because you can still get good ones for a fair price

Italian
Italians wines are mainly good with food. Chianti in Borgo was great with the roast dinner. Barolo courtesy of Anthony was nice. Definitely had a Tabacco aroma, and was better with the food

Rosso di montepulciano was great in Trattoria Rosmarino
chianti was great with steak in 2025 Matt’s flat near Parma


Tannins are part of the fruits defences, in humans they bind to our saliva and make the mouth less lubricated

ingredients/scallop.title: scallop
ingredients/scallop.content:
when buying frozen, the only ingredient should be scallop (not strange chemicals to retain water) 
                  
                

At Queen’s Park farmers market, £48/kg
So £9 for 6

Uncover to dry out and season
Ripping hot pan (stainless steel or carbon steel)
Add high smoke point oil
When smoking add scallops and press down and season this side too
Cook for 90 secs ish
Flip and cook for 60 secs
54C inside



                  
                  try poaching in butter like on that Munchies Scotland episode 
                  
                

Try this recipe




                  
                  Warning
                  
                

Don’t use a stainless steel pan!
Don’t add too much oil


Go well with leeks as in this recipe
facts
Pg 224 On food and Cooking, have two adductor muscles to close the shell. One fast twitch muscle for closing the shell quickly against predators, and one smaller one with slow twitch cells to hold it together for days at a time. This but is not all cut off
This is also the muscle that weakens when cooked and why Moules marinière open











FileChorizo scallop pastacevichescallop leek chorizo gratinGreat British menuEDENJumbo CrabOsteria del LovoThe OXO tower

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.content:
This site uses Clicky for basic analytics — it tells me which pages are popular so I know what’s worth writing up.

It’s configured cookie-free: nothing is stored on your device.
It does not track you across other websites.
It does not collect personal information. Only aggregate data like page views and approximate location (from an anonymised IP) is used, purely to understand general traffic.

No ads, no third-party trackers, nothing sold. Questions? Reach me via ronanlaker.com.

recipes/bolognese.title: Bolognese
recipes/bolognese.content:
Watch technique here

from Kenji
Old Gino recipe
This is my fav recipe based on one from Gino but with a few tricks from Kenji
Serves 4 for Bolognese plus 2 Lasagnes!
Ingredients
ingredients

 2 tablespoons olive oil
 1 large onion, very finely diced
 4 celery sticks, very finely diced
 2 large carrot, very finely diced
 500g minced pork
 500g minced beef
 500g lamb mince
 (optional) 1/2 pack of pancetta

recipes/christmas-dinner.title: Christmas dinner
recipes/christmas-dinner.content:
The perfect Christmas dinner is much more than the food, its about the company, the craic and a healthy dose of nostalgia - but it can’t hurt to try and perfect the food anyway…
In addition to all the tips learnt from Roast dinners, Christmas dinners need to be completed with at a scale no British oven was meant to handle. The mentality should be more akin to Escoffier’s military brigade than a family friendly affair
Operation Yuletide
Christmas Eve

Butcher bird into spatchcock, or just take off the legs and a crown. Dry brine the bird by salting liberally under the skin and leaving uncovered in the fridge overnight - The food lab
Make the Cauliflower cheese and put into the fridge
Make the base gravy using the trimmings from the bird
Make the stuffing

Christmas Day
Targeting a 14:00 finish

11:00 - peel and cut the potatoes, carrots, brussel sprouts and parsnips. Cut roast potatoes into a variety of sizes for heterogeneity and have more diced potatoes for the Mashed potato (store in water until later)
12:30  - mix together butter, lemon zest, garlic and thyme and place under the skin of the bird. Put into the oven uncovered to brown the skin
12:35  - boil roast potatoes and parsnips for 15 mins to gelatinize the outside starch which will then go crispy as it is fried in oil
12:45 - put goose fat in oven until smoking
12:50 - drain roast potatoes and shake in the colander. Leave to sit for 10 mins to dry out
13:00 - take goose fat out of oven and pour some out so that it can be ladled over the top of the fragile potatoes. Add potatoes and return to the oven in the hottest place
13:00 also add the carrots and parsnips, can be warmed later
13:20 - put pigs in blankets, stuffing and cauliflower cheese into the oven
13:25 - boil diced potatoes for mash, about 16 mins but depends on the size of the dice
13:30 - check temperature of bird and remove from oven. Aiming for 68C white meat rising to 74C after resting, 90C dark meat. Either carve legs off and leave in the oven, or give them a head start. Wrap in kitchen foil and rest for 30 mins
13:30 - Get the gravy on the hob and start warming up. Add in the chicken juices and season with lemon juice/port
13:30 - start frying the brussel sprouts on the hob with butter
13:45 - warm serving dishes in oven (or soak in hot water and dry)
13:45 - open the wine to let it breathe
13:50 - Mash the potato and whisk in butter and milk for the right texture
13:55 - carve the bird from the crown then slice across the grain and set on serving dish. Separate leg portions and arrange on the plate too
14:00 - Serve


Ingredients
ingredients

  Chicken (can rest for 30 mins)
 butter
 lemon
 garlic
 thyme
 potatoes
 goose fat
 brussel sprouts
 carrot (fancy)
 parsnip
 Cauliflower cheese (make ahead)
 gravy (make ahead)
 Stuffing
 pigs in blankets

Restaurants

recipes/roast-chicken-dinner.content:
Adam Ragusea 
says roast in a stainless steel pan!


                  
                  Success
                  
                

just buy an organic one, they taste better. Dry aging in the fridge overnight gets a leathery skin that shatters like glass


Throw in some unpeeled garlic and shallots like Nigella - How to eat for some extra flavour
Leftovers could be:

Chicken, leek and chorizo pie
Chicken pot pie
Egg fried rice
One pot creamy chicken spinach (orzo)

recipes/steak.title: Steak
recipes/steak.content:
sides

tomatoes, avocado, Coriander, lime salsa
corn on the cob
red pepper
grilled onion
roast potatoes and gravy for an Germanic vibe


Videos
BBQ
Coals need to be super hot - never try and save coals by doing half a chimney it needs the full thing so it stacks high enough and retains heat. By the end we should be charting the grates like we did on the Tomahawk. It smelt like steak for days after!
So when you tried thin steaks a few days later the temperature simply wasn’t hot enough, especially since the steaks were thin and would have needed a cast iron to be ripping hot! See this video. Need super high heat but also keep moving the steaks about to get even cooking
Temperature should be 300C when thermometer over coals (?)
Good quick overview - put steaks on the cold side of the grill then rotate it. That way they don’t get grill lines in some places and underdone in other. Instead you end up with a consistent crust rathe
Fun short showing way to use the chimney to sear then finish off with the BBQ
For a large steak, sear then cool side with lid on until target temp (40C-45C)

Pan
Quick guide to temperatures
For Rib-eye take off when 45C for me, 40C for Liv



Thin steak

Salt meat 40-60 mins before hand. Pat down before frying
Heat pan really hot
Place steaks and don’t move for 1.5-2 mins
Flip and another 1.5-2 mins
Don’t cut up meat for thin steaks as it goes cold
add finishing salt to cut meat
Bon appetite

Thick steak
Sear then oven
This is traditional but is hard to execute
2-3 mins each side then oven for 6 mins (like Pan fried Duck breast)
Cold sear
Start off cold and keep flipping
Reverse sear
Put in oven first on really low temperature (like 120C, which makes the temp even throughout
Take out when around 40C
sear for flavour

Facts
Pg 133 of On food and Cooking
Myoglobin can be three colours:

red when attached to an oxygen
Purple when oxygen is taken away
Brown when attached to H2O

So vacuum packed meat is purple and blooms into red when opened. When meat is fresh it has the enzymes still present so stays red, but these degrade turning the meat brown
The link colour of cured meat (like Italian sausage in 2025 Matt’s flat near Parma) is also due to myoglobin reacting with salt. As is the pink ring from BBQ food, this is myoglobin interacting with small amounts of Nitrous oxide generated by the fire
Pg 149 of On food and Cooking

ingredients

 Garlic
 Butter
 Cherry tomatoes
 Red pepper
 corn on the cob

recipes/braised-beef.title: braised beef
recipes/braised-beef.content:
Look in The Book of St John pg 31, similar to osso bucco better than Braised beef ragu
Can also apply to pork shoulder or lamb shoulder
cider braised pork
From pg 163 of On food and Cooking, want to braise just above the collagen dissolving temperature of 70-80C. A sign this has happened is that the meat is still red inside (because other proteins have been denatured so myoglobin interacts differently)

Ingredients
ingredients

  Beef shin or oxtail
 1 onion (quartered)
 4 garlic whole
 Two sprigs of rosemary
 Equal of thyme
 Couple bay leaves for depth
 1/3 bottle of red wine

recipes/chicken-pan-sauce.title: chicken pan sauce
recipes/chicken-pan-sauce.content:
Many different variations of this, but follow the same rough steps
Short
Fry Chicken and remove from pan as well as most of the fat
add shallots, garlic and butter to get fond off the bottom
Sweat down then add brandy or white wine and reduce until almost sticky (can flame if you want to show off)
Add chicken stock and reduce again until nice and thick
Add dash of cream and Dijon mustard
Or add in cold butter and mix well

Adding cornstarch gives a safety barrier because starch gels at 77C and absorbs water which slows the temperature down! Pg 94 of On food and Cooking
Custards need dissolved minerals in the liquid. I.e. milk or chicken stock but plain water will not work

views/protein.title: Protein
views/protein.content:
Meat
Stressing an animal before slaughter does impact the quality of the meat. Normally, the cell muscles continue to use up the remaining resources and create lactic acid even after death. If you stress an animal before then all this energy is used up and the lack of lactic acid will result in faster spoilage
Pg 142 of On food and Cooking
